body:
## Bug: All agents receive all tools regardless of configuration

OpenClaw documentation states agents should be narrow and focused. However, the default tool configuration gives every agent the full kitchen sink — all 34 tools available to every agent by default, with no filtering.

## Evidence

The active-memory memory search sub-agent prompt (via runEmbeddedPiAgent) includes all tools: read, write, edit, exec, process, web_search, web_fetch, browser, canvas, message, agents_list, sessions_list, sessions_history, sessions_send, subagents, session_status, image, image_generate, memory_get, memory_search, music_generate, pdf, sessions_spawn, sessions_yield, tts, video_generate, wiki_apply, wiki_get, wiki_lint, wiki_search, wiki_status.

This for an agent that only needs memory_search and memory_get.

## Specific absurdity

- wiki_apply, wiki_get, wiki_lint, wiki_search, wiki_status are Obsidian wiki tools (optional plugin) yet appear in every agent's default tool list
- music_generate and video_generate appear for memory search and other agents that have no use for them
- The agent prompt tells the model to only use 2 specific tools while showing it 34 tools

## Expected behavior

Least-privilege: tools should be disabled by default, enabled explicitly per-agent configuration. Narrow by default, expanded as needed.

## Impact

- Token bloat: 5-10x more prompt tokens than needed
- Higher costs and slower generation
- Actively contradicts documented narrow-agent philosophy

Related: #66060 (prompt bloat in active-memory)
title: Agent tool permissions: every agent gets all tools by default, contradicting narrow-agent philosophy
